key_insight: The paper adds computable two-sided a posteriori error certificates for PINNs solving ODEs by pairing new lower bounds with sharper localized upper bounds, all without needing the exact solution.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable deployment of PINN-based solvers in engineering workflows by quantifying when predictions are provably accurate enough for safety- or certification-sensitive tasks.
method: Derive lower error bounds from localized strong monotonicity on certified state-space domains.
Derive complementary upper bounds using a one-sided Lipschitz condition and residual-based estimates.
For linear systems, express certificates via eigenvalues of the symmetric part; use signed-residual finite probes when exact initial conditions make scalar lower bounds trivial.
evidence_signal: The abstract presents a theory-and-formula-based framework for ODEs, including explicit linear-system certificates and a certificate-informed training strategy, but does not report numerical experiments or benchmark results.
watch_item: The guarantees are domain- and assumption-dependent, so monotonicity/Lipschitz conditions and certified state-space regions must be verified before the bounds are meaningful.
paper_title: Reliable Error Estimation for PINNs: Lower and Upper A Posteriori Bounds
